title: Slovakian Semolina-Rimacinata Pierogi
description: Comforting semolina-rimacinata pierogi from Slovakia, perfect for a cozy night in.

ingredients.name: semolina flour
ingredients.notes: use a fine or medium grind

ingredients.name: ricotta cheese
ingredients.notes: whole milk or skim milk work equally well

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: whole milk or skim milk work equally well

instructions: Combine semolina flour and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Add eggs, one at a time, mixing until dough forms.
Knead dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Wrap dough in plastic wrap and rest for 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix ricotta cheese and melted butter in a separate bowl.
Add milk, mixing until smooth and creamy.
Add water as needed to achieve a thick but pourable consistency.
Roll out each piece into a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon or two of filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold dough over filling, pressing edges to seal.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Cook pierogi in batches, about 5-7 minutes per batch, or until they float to the surface.
Remove cooked pierogi with a slotted spoon and transfer to a serving dish.
Serve hot with melted butter and a sprinkle of chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
